mnemonics.title: Clean Architecture Layers
mnemonics.content: DDP: DATA -> DOMAIN -> PRESENTATION - Data flows outward, dependencies point inward.

mnemonics.title: MVVM Components
mnemonics.content: MVP: MODEL (data) -> VIEW (UI) -> VIEWMODEL (binds them together)

mnemonics.title: Repository Purpose
mnemonics.content: RAPID: Repository Abstracts Power Into Data - provides unified API for different data sources

acronyms.usage: Android UI architecture pattern separating UI from business logic
Architecture pattern where Presenter handles user interactions
Traditional pattern with Controller handling user input
Pattern for providing dependencies from external sources
Pattern for abstracting database operations in Room
Single task encapsulation in Clean Architecture

visualTips.visualization: Think of an OREO COOKIE: Presentation is the top cookie, Domain is the cream filling (core), Data is the bottom cookie - with dependencies pointing inward toward the cream.
Think of a RESTAURANT: ViewModel is the Waiter, Model is the Kitchen (prepares food/data), View is the Customer (sees and interacts with the final dish).
Think of a LIBRARY CATALOG: The repository is like a librarian who knows where every book (data) is - whether in the stacks (local DB) or through interlibrary loan (network).

analogies.analogy: ViewModel is like a TV remote - it holds the current state and controls what you see, surviving when you change channels (configuration changes).
LiveData is like a NEWS ALERT subscription - it automatically notifies you when something changes without you constantly checking.
Repository is like a HOTEL CONCIERGE - they handle all the complex arrangements behind the scenes while you just ask for what you need.
UseCase is like a RECIPE - it's a specific set of instructions for one particular dish (task) that the kitchen (domain) follows.
